name: form-control
role: Polymorphic form field primitive — composes label + widget + help text + error message into a single accessible unit. The `type` attribute determines the widget rendered: text, email, password, search, number, url, tel (single-line inputs); textarea (multi-line); select (native dropdown); combobox (typeahead / autocomplete); checkbox; radio; switch; file; date. All 8 microstates supported across every widget type. Composes from vocab-forms primitives (form-field, field-label, field-help, field-error, checkbox-cell, radio-cell, select-cell, textarea-cell).

semanticStructure: <label for="<input-id>"> — required for every form-control (visible label OR aria-label/aria-labelledby)
<input|select|textarea> — the actual widget; native HTML element, not a div-pretending-to-be-an-input
<small> or <span class="help"> — optional help text below the widget; aria-describedby links input to it
<span role="alert" class="error"> — optional error message; appears when state=error; aria-describedby links input to it

widgetMapping.text|email|password|search|number|url|te: renders <input type="<type>"> with native browser validation hints
widgetMapping.textarea: renders <textarea> with rows=4 default; resize: vertical
widgetMapping.select: renders native <select> (not a custom div-dropdown); for searchable use type=combobox instead
widgetMapping.combobox: renders <input role="combobox" aria-autocomplete="list" aria-controls="<listbox-id>"> + a <ul role="listbox"> popover; supports keyboard nav, Enter/Tab to select, Esc to close. ARIA-conformant per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices 1.3 combobox pattern.
widgetMapping.checkbox: renders <input type="checkbox"> with custom visual styling but native semantics; supports indeterminate via aria-checked="mixed"
widgetMapping.radio: renders <input type="radio"> inside a <fieldset> if multiple options; legend provides group label
widgetMapping.switch: renders <input type="checkbox" role="switch" aria-checked="…"> with switch visual treatment; per WAI-ARIA 1.3 the underlying control is still a checkbox semantically
widgetMapping.file: renders <input type="file"> with custom visual wrapper that triggers the native file picker (clicking the wrapper proxies to the hidden input); preserves drag-and-drop semantics
widgetMapping.date: renders <input type="date"> with native browser date picker; fallback to text input on browsers without date picker support

meta.minHitTarget: 44x44 CSS pixels for md and lg sizes; sm reserved for inline / dense table-row contexts where parent row provides hit area

leadingTrailingPattern.icon: 16px SVG positioned inside the input padding zone; non-interactive; aria-hidden=true unless functional
leadingTrailingPattern.addon: static text or short label (e.g. 'https://' prefix, '.com' suffix, '$' currency); rendered as a separate <span> inside the input border
leadingTrailingPattern.clear: trailing X button to clear the input; appears only when input has value; aria-label='Clear <field-name>'
leadingTrailingPattern.reveal: trailing eye-icon button for password fields; toggles input type between password and text; aria-pressed indicates current state

states.default: no visible validation state; resting interactive
states.validating: spinner replaces trailing icon; aria-busy='true' on input; aria-live='polite' for async validation feedback
states.error: border-color: --critical; error message visible; input aria-invalid='true'; aria-describedby points to error message
states.success: border-color: --success; optional checkmark trailing icon; aria-describedby points to success message

microStates.disabled: [disabled] — opacity 0.5, pointer-events: none, native disabled attribute on input
microStates.loading: [aria-busy=true] — spinner overlay; preserves visible width; preserves value visibility
microStates.read-only: [readonly] — value visible but not editable; background --surface-recessed; cursor: default

ariaPatterns.labelLinking: <input id> + <label for> + aria-describedby (help+error) is the canonical wiring. Generate stable IDs (uuid or content-hash) so SSR is stable.
ariaPatterns.errorAnnouncement: Error message has role='alert' so screen readers announce when it appears. Also aria-live='polite' so dynamic validation announces.
ariaPatterns.comboboxKeyboard: ArrowDown opens listbox + focuses first option; ArrowUp/Down navigate; Enter selects + closes; Esc closes; Tab confirms current + advances focus; Home/End jump to first/last.
ariaPatterns.switchAria: role='switch' + aria-checked='true|false'. NOT aria-pressed (that's for toggle buttons).
ariaPatterns.fileInputCustomTrigger: The visual wrapper is button-like but clicking it proxies to the hidden <input type=file>. The native input retains focus order; visual wrapper has tabindex='-1' to avoid double-tab.
ariaPatterns.indeterminateCheckbox: Setting checkbox.indeterminate=true via JS reflects to aria-checked='mixed'. Common pattern: select-all checkbox above a list.

meta.reducedMotion: spinner animation collapses to non-animated 'Loading…' label when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce; focus transitions become instant

name: form-row
role: Composition primitive for laying out multiple form-controls horizontally. Two registers — grid (CSS Grid with fr-based columns, useful for fixed-width labels OR multi-column form layouts) and inline (flex-row, useful for compact prompt + input + button rows). Handles responsive collapse to single-column at <640px viewport. Inherits density from parent form-section.

registers.grid: grid-template-columns: repeat(<columns>, 1fr) at ≥640px; collapses to 1fr below. columns=auto uses minmax(min(280px, 100%), 1fr) for adaptive fill.
registers.inline: flex-direction: row; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: end (so labels at varying heights align by their input baseline); gap: --space-3.

responsiveBreakpoints.mobile: <640px: all rows collapse to single column regardless of declared columns count

name: form-section
role: Top-level form wrapper grouping related form-controls under a section heading. Provides the form-level state machine (default / validating / error / success / submitting), the submit-row at the bottom, and the surrounding ARIA contract (aria-label or aria-labelledby on the <form>). Pattern-pack-level state attribute is read here, not on individual form-controls.

stateBehavior.default: normal interactive state; controls respond to input
stateBehavior.validating: async validation in progress on at least one field; submit button disabled; controls themselves remain editable so the user can correct in-flight
stateBehavior.error: at least one field has state=error; submit button enabled (so retry is one click) but emits aria-describedby to the form-level error summary
stateBehavior.success: form submitted successfully; controls become read-only; success message + 'Continue' or 'Submit another' action
stateBehavior.loading: form data fetching (e.g. edit-mode populating from server); skeleton placeholders for each control; submit button hidden
stateBehavior.submitting: POST in flight; all controls disabled; submit button shows loading state; aria-busy='true' on form

ariaPatterns.formLabel: <form aria-labelledby='<section-heading-id>'> — section heading is the form label
ariaPatterns.errorSummary: When form is in error state, render a single error summary at the top with role='alert'. Summary lists which fields have errors and links to them via in-page anchors. WCAG 2.4.6 + 3.3.1 + 3.3.3 compliant.
ariaPatterns.submittingLiveRegion: aria-live='polite' region announces 'Submitting…' / 'Saved' / 'Error: …' as the form state transitions

meta.noscriptFallback: form-section is a real <form> so it submits via POST even without JS. JS enhancement adds async validation + optimistic UI; the no-JS path is functional baseline.
meta.deepLinkability: form fields' values should be deep-linkable when appropriate (e.g. search-filter forms preserve filter state in URL hash or query params). Implementation is consumer responsibility; the pattern provides the wrapper.
